name: No-Tomato Chili Con Carne y Frijoles
recipeIngredient: 3 Tablespoons Chili Powder, More As Needed
2 Cans Pinto Beans or Red Beans, Drained (15 or 16 Ounces Each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the meat in a Dutch oven or heavy pot. Drain any excess grease.
2. Add minced garlic, diced onion, and diced bell pepper to the pan. Sauté until slightly softened.
3. Stir in flour, cooking until lightly browned. Scrape the sides of the pan to incorporate all the bits.
4. Add oregano, paprika, cumin, chili powder, black pepper, cayenne pepper, and salt. Mix thoroughly.
5. Pour in beef broth or stock (or water with 4 bouillon cubes). Bring to a boil and simmer for about 45 minutes.
6. Stir in the drained beans and bring back to a simmer until heated through, approximately 10 minutes.
7. Add crushed soda crackers to achieve the desired consistency. Be careful not to over-thicken.
8. Allow the chili to sit for several hours or overnight to allow flavors to blend. Reheat if necessary.
9. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed.
This recipe freezes well.
